design_philosophy.core_loop: Fly omnidirectionally while automatically scrolling through stages, shoot left/right with dual orbiting satellites providing supplementary fire, manage power-ups and hit points across five stages with boss encounters
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource attrition (hit points, weapon drops) vs. enemy density; satellite positioning strategy vs. movement freedom; risk/reward of pursuing power-up containers
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Piloting a cybernetically enhanced soldier through alien-infested environments with satellite support, wielding exotic weapons (laser, wide beam, bomb launcher) to defend Earth from extraterrestrial invasion
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Asymmetric control scheme: eight-directional flight but only left/right firing; satellite positioning as core mechanic rather than passive enhancement; strict weapon loss penalties (JP version) create tension around power-up collection; limited hit points force cautious play despite aggressive aesthetics

difficulty.lives_system: JP version: 3 hit points per life; NA/EU versions: 6 hit points per life. Each hit reduces HP by 1. Game over when all HP depleted.
difficulty.continues_system: Unlimited continues available; player can retry stages without restriction
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing; player restarts at beginning of current stage upon game over
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Regional difficulty options (JP harder than NA/EU); weapon retention mechanic differs by region (JP loses all weapons on hit vs. NA/EU loses only on game over); satellite behavior can be locked in position for strategic advantage; power-up collection strategy affects survivability

progression.upgrade_system: Five collectible power-ups accessed via destroying containers: three weapon replacements (Laser, Wide Beam, Bomb Launcher), speed boost, and life recovery. Weapons are temporary; replaced when collecting new weapon or lost on damage (JP) / game over (NA/EU)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Five stages unlock sequentially; cheat codes allow stage skip (level select codes for stages 2-5 in JP, stages 2-4 in NA/EU versions)
replayability_hooks: Two-player simultaneous co-op with distinct character selections (Arnold/Sigourney in NA/EU; palette-swapped soldiers in JP)
Regional version differences incentivize replaying (JP version significantly harder, different difficulty curve)
Multiple weapon types encourage different tactical approaches
High score tracking with score presentation screen before each stage (NA/EU versions)

world_building_techniques: Opening story sequence showing city obliteration before alien invasion (JP version more elaborate than NA/EU)
Character naming with actor reference (Arnold Schwarzenegger / Sigourney Weaver parallels in NA version; renamed to Silver Man/Sparks in EU)
Stage-based map progression showing future levels (NA/EU version feature; shows upcoming environments)
Sci-fi premise: year 2029, part-human/part-machine soldiers, Astrotube connection to alien space station
Villain identification: Vile Malmort as invasion leader
Location framing: New York City ruins as primary invasion point

boss_design.philosophy: Stage-ending encounters with thematic enemy designs (battleship boss, astrotube facility boss, etc.); bosses feature destructible components with visual feedback (damaged nozzles graphics show progression)
notable_bosses: Level 3 Boss: Features beam attacks and destructible nozzle components (unused graphics variants suggest iterative design with multiple beam and damage states)

faction_indicators: S.C.A.T. (Special Cybernetic Attack Team) vs. alien invaders led by Vile Malmort
Team affiliation changed regionally: SCAT (JP/NA) vs. SAT/Special Attack Team (EU)
Part-human, part-machine soldier identity as faction marker
danger_signals: Obliteration of multiple cities in opening sequence
Astrotube connection enabling direct extraterrestrial access to Earth
Enemy container deployment and satellite opposition forces
environmental_storytelling: Ruins of New York City as invasion staging ground
Space station presence as visible threat
Stage environments (battleship, astrotube facility, etc.) show military/technological infrastructure under attack
Progressive alien structure infiltration across five stages

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - S.C.A.T. article (regional differences, unused graphics, cheat codes, development data)
Wikipedia - S.C.A.T.: Special Cybernetic Attack Team (gameplay mechanics, plot, version differences, reception)
